matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: AR CAG-repeat model (AR polyglutamine-length modulation model)
AR ligand-binding-domain resistance model (AR endocrine-resistance model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AR androgen receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response and Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed AR CAG-repeat model dosage suggests this sample carries the AR prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a AR androgen receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response and Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as AR polyglutamine-length modulation model. AR CAG-repeat model is curated as a AR marker for Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched AR CAG-repeat model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the AR prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AR androgen receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response and Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. An observed AR variant places this sample in the curated AR research context. The local AR bundle is androgen receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. AR variants can alter or tag androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a AR variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AR androgen receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response and Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. AR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local AR bundle is androgen receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. AR variants can alter or tag androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
